rationale: Pairwise contradiction scan of Requirements + ACs + Non-Goals. The four flagged pairs all resolve cleanly by explicit construction: (1) R7's advisory-skip is conditioned on gates: being configured; gates-absent falls back to 109's global compare, so R2's byte-identity is preserved, not violated. (2) Per-gate override strictly precedes global (R3 chain gate->adhoc->global->defaults); nothing asserts the reverse, and R2's global path applies only when no per-gate value exists. (3) substitutes supersedes claude_sub_on_quota only while non-empty; empty preserves the 109 meaning; DefaultConfig (bool true + empty substitutes) is consistent — one policy in force, decidable by inspection. (4) The closed-enum gates: config keys (R1/R4 refuse unknown) vs the parse-lenient recorded gate field (R6, unexpected value surfaced not rejected, to avoid fail-open) are two distinct surfaces; strict-config/lenient-record is coherent, not contradictory. One residual seam (non-blocking, advisory-only, decision-inert): R6 permits an unexpected recorded gate value but R7 does not map the recorded-but-unknown case, and R3's PanelGateExpectedReviewers errors on unknown gate names. Resolvable at plan time (treat unknown-recorded-gate as no-recorded-gate for the advisory). No hard mutual contradiction found.

findings.summary: R6/R7/R3 seam: R6 allows a parse-lenient, possibly-unknown recorded `gate` value on panel.json, but R7 only specifies two record-side branches (recorded KNOWN gate -> PanelGateExpectedReviewers(gate); NO recorded gate). A recorded-but-unknown gate is unspecified for R7, and the only resolver R7 would call is specified in R3 to ERROR on an unknown gate name. Advisory-only and decision-inert (worst case a missing/spurious note, never a wrong Allow/Block), and it sits within R7's existing 'known gate' guard. Recommend the plan pin R7's behavior for a recorded-but-unknown gate (fall through to the no-recorded-gate branch), and add an AC probing it. Not a blocker.

Minor coherence note (not a contradiction): R1 says a family-only entry 'resolves its model to the family string' without pinning WHEN (load-time struct value vs resolve-time), which lightly interacts with R2/AC7's 'every field equal to 109' and R1's gates round-trip falsification. No requirement pair conflicts; flagging for the implementability lens (G1) to disambiguate load-time vs resolve-time derivation so legacy {family} entries round-trip unchanged.
